steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the text 'most strongly suggests' about Tom Towers. The key sentence is: 'This kind of hidden but still conscious glory suited the nature of the man.' He sits silently, listens to politicians, and thinks about how they are 'all in his power.' The answer must match this portrait of quiet, private awareness of his own influence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'He has received much praise but tends to place too much value on his own importance': the text describes 'hidden' glory, not public praise; and there is no suggestion his self-assessment is inflated beyond what is warranted. ✗
  • Choice B — 'He takes private satisfaction in his capacity to influence public opinion': sitting silently while thinking 'how they all were in his power' is exactly private satisfaction in quiet influence — a direct match. ✓
  • Choice C — 'He aims mainly to win the regard of readers who reside in other provinces': the mention of far-off provinces describes how widely The Jupiter is read, not Towers's personal ambition toward those readers. ✗
  • Choice D — 'He flatters himself that his readers take a greater interest in him than in politicians': the text shows Towers thinking about his power over politicians, not comparing his readers' interest in him versus politicians. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
